body:
Mum, we will come by around one on Sunday as usual. Lily wants to show you her library book and Jack has lost another tooth.

Can I bring anything? I can pick up bread on the way.

Beth

body:
Sunday at one is fine. Do not worry about bread, I have plenty.

Tell Lily I have set aside the Anne of Green Gables for her. I will make soup.

Love, Mum

body:
Dear volunteers,

The spring used-book sale is approaching. We are organising shifts for the second and fourth Saturday mornings, nine to noon.

If you can take a sorting shift, please reply with your availability.

With thanks,
Cranston Public Library Volunteer Office

Good morning,

Kind regards,
Samantha Long

body:
This is a reminder of your appointment for routine bloodwork with Dr. Anwar.

- Date: Thursday 14 May 2026
- Time: 10:00 AM
- Location: Edgewood Medical Associates, Cranston

Please fast for twelve hours beforehand. Reply or call the office to reschedule.

body:
Sam, are we still on for Chelo's on Friday at half eleven? I want to hear how the garden plot is coming along.

My knee has been complaining but the walk does it good.

Frank

Samantha

body:
Members,

A few notes for the season:

- Plots must be cleared of winter debris by the end of the month.
- The water line will be turned on once the risk of frost has passed.
- Plot 14 and its neighbours: please mind the shared path.

Happy gardening,
Edgewood Community Garden

body:
Dear Ms. Long,

Your prescription for hydroxychloroquine is ready for collection at the pharmacy. It will be held for fourteen days.

Thank you,
Cranston Pharmacy

body:
Hi Mum, I will ring you on the first Saturday at two your time as usual. Work has been busy but good. Denver finally has some sun.

How is the neuropathy treating you?

Tom

Saturday at two is fine, I will be home. The hands are no worse, the new gabapentin dose helps in the evenings.

Take your coat, I read it was cold there.

Love, Mum
